title: Use lap-snapshot peer residuals, not only race/compound medians
summary: Undercut and pit-window pressure are relative phenomena: fresh-tyre pace, in-lap weakness, and degradation matter versus other cars on the same lap under the same track evolution.
rationale: Undercut and pit-window pressure are relative phenomena: fresh-tyre pace, in-lap weakness, and degradation matter versus other cars on the same lap under the same track evolution. Current preprocessing only residualizes by whole race or compound, which is too coarse.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x LapNumber` and `Race_Year x LapNumber x slick/wet` snapshot, compute percentile or robust z-score versions of `LapTime (s)`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Position_Change`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and `Deg_Per_TyreLife`; then add 1-3 lap rolling changes of those residuals inside each driver-race sequence.
expected_effect: Should surface cliff/in-lap behavior and local pace collapse without adding more fixed tyre-life constants.
risk: Lagged residuals must remain strictly prior-only within each driver-race sequence to avoid leakage.
